Imports subtrees (#158)

Adds a new macro `\agda-imports` for a folded-by-default subtree with the imports of each module.
The agda imports block should not contain imports that rename entries, `Axiom` imports, or definitions.

The only agda files that do not have an agda imports block now are:

```text
src/Axioms/UF.lagda.tree
src/Foundations/Universes.lagda.tree
src/Foundations/Prelude.lagda.tree
src/Foundations/Reasoning.lagda.tree
```

Additionally,
- Minimizes the foundations imports that happen before the main module declaration, to make it easier to see what's needed for the module declaration itself.
- Removes the distributivity assumption on lattices where not needed, which is everywhere.
- Assumes a cohesive interval also in `Synthetic.Categories.UniversalElements`, like everywhere else.
- Removes some redundant imports and sorts the `Foundations.Prelude` file.

Builds on #156. Resolves #64.

chore: fix CI triggers (#176)

Pre-commit no longer triggers on pushes to arbitrary branches in this
repo in addition to on pushes to pull requests. Also, currently
pre-commit and build triggers on draft pull requests. This changes it so
they only run once it is marked as ready for review.

bug: Fix `\agda-imports` macro name (#178)

I had forgotten to rename the actual macro in #158, this fixes that.

As an aside, it's strange to me that forester does not pick up on this
with an error.

Updates renamer (#172)

Some improvements to the tree renamer:

- [x] Adds makefile target, `make list-trees` that uses rgrep and find,
together with awk to list all the trees in the project.
- [x] Updates the Python script to use this utility rather than calling
`forester` to get a list of trees.
 - [x] Add a makefile target for renaming trees (#171):
   - Checks that there are no duplicate trees
   - Checks that the repo is synchronised with upstream/main
   - Checks that the repo is in a clean state
   - Displays the ouput a dry run, and asks for confirmation
   - Then performs the renaming
   - Optionally makes a git commit - enabled by default
- [x] Add some contributor documentation for how to name trees and use
the renamer etc.

This partially (or maybe completely?) addresses #113:

> The tree ID renamer script suffers from a series of issues which makes
it impractical to work with, and that may be improved.


> - Forester build dependency. Currently the script uses the build
artifacts of forester, which is not great for multiple reasons:
> 1. It requires that `forester` is installed, which we ultimately don't
want. [Get rid of user-side `forester` dependency
#114](https://github.com/samtoth/agda-synthetic-categories/issues/114)
> 2. It requires that the project is built before the script can run,
adding an extra step to the process of renaming IDs.
>       3. This solution is fragile wrt. dirty repo states.

I think this change fixes all of these issues, `forester` is no longer a
dependency of the tree renamer, and the forest does not need to be build
- or even buildable for this to work.


> - Python dependency. It might be possible to implement the same
functionality with `sed` (which ships with unix based systems (TODO:
also document alternative for windows users(WSL?)))

The Python dependency is still there. Maybe when I have time I can try
and port the whole thing to bash, but since we have a dependency on
Python now via `pre-commit` I don't think this is a top priority.

> - Every subtree must be manually ID'd. An alternative we've discussed
is to autofill unID'd subtrees of the form `\subtree[]{` with the script
(note the empty `[...]`).

This is still an issue; however I think this could be solved by having
tooling to automatically generate a new unique random ID in the editor
for example. I don't think the proposed solution (of having blank
subtrees) is ideal since we still want to be able to reference the
subtrees before the final renaming.

> - There seems to be performance issues with the current implementation
where it takes multiple minutes to run.

Whilst I never encountered this with the last system - I suspect it was
caused by forester itself having to build the forest before being able
to generate the forest JSON data. This new system circumvents forester
so this shouldn't be an issue.

Lattice structure on simplices (#199)

Defines the pointwise lattice structure on the inductively-defined
n-simplices as a small first step towards #188.
The opposite and (co)slice of a lattice  (#203)

Simplify the definition of meet on the 2-simplex (#201)

Currently, the definition of multiplication in the 2-simplex lattice is
polynomial multiplication: (a, b) ∧ (c, d) = (bc ∨ ad ∨ ac, bd).
However, since in this case a ≥ b and c ≥ d, the simpler pointwise
definition (a, b) ∧ (c, d) = (ac, bd) is equivalent. Using the other
definition makes the proof that Δ² is a lattice much simpler, and so far
doesn't seem to make anything else more complicated.

Co-authored-by: Fredrik Bakke <fredrbak@gmail.com>

Basics of cofinal maps (#142)

- ~[x] Defines cocartesian fibrations~
- ~[ ] A cocartesian fibration is a left fibration iff all arrows are
cocartesian~
 - [x] Defines left Cofinal maps
 - ~[ ] Defines contravariant equivalences~
- [x] Left fibrations are those maps which induce equivalences on slice
categories
- [x] Left cofinal maps are stable under base change by right fibrations
 - ~[ ] Left cofinal maps are initial~
 - ~[ ] A map is Left cofinal iff it is a left fibration~
 - ~[ ] The universal property of the free left fibration~
 - ~[ ] Dualise everything~

Partially addresses #117 and #109 

Trying to get enough facts about cofinal maps down to finish off the
hole in the proof of directed univalence. I started with some
definitions of cocartesian fibrations because the proof that left
Cofinal maps are left anodyne goes via the free fibration. It might be
nice if this can be avoided since it seems like it will require more
machinery around cocartesian fibrations.

As for terminology, I propose the following:

- Left cofinal for the condition that the generalised slices are
groupoid connected
 - Left anodyne for being left orthogonal to all left fibrations
- Initial for inducing equivalences on the coslice categories (i.e.
preserving colimits)
- Covariant equivalences for .... (I need to understand this condition a
bit better...)

Narrowing the scope of this PR down to just include defining left
Cofinal maps, and a few basic facts

Conjunctive types (#209)

Dualises disjunctive types, using the opposite lattice. Note that in
this PR we do not show simplicial types are conjunctive. This we
postpone until #208 is implemented, which would make this proof trivial
too.
